name: OneStream, Inc.

profile.description: OneStream delivers a unified, AI-enabled enterprise finance platform that replaces fragmented legacy tools for financial close, consolidation, planning, budgeting, forecasting, and ESG reporting. The company's Digital Finance Cloud platform serves over 1,700 customers including 18% of Fortune 500 companies and was recognized as a 5x Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader. OneStream was acquired by Hg Capital in a $6.4B all-cash deal ($24/share) that closed on April 1, 2026; the stock is no longer publicly traded on NASDAQ.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): OneStream grew revenue from $279M in 2022 to $602M in 2025, a 29% 3-year CAGR, by displacing legacy CPM tools from Oracle Hyperion and SAP BPC with a unified cloud platform [1]. The company secured 5 consecutive Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader positions and was placed furthest in vision in the 2026 Gartner MQ for Financial Close. AI Deployment (2/2): SensibleAI portfolio is shipping to customers with measurable results — AI bookings grew 60% YoY in the first nine months of 2025 [2][3]. SensibleAI Agents, SensibleAI Studio (30+ plug-and-play AI routines), and SensibleAI Forecast provide no-code ML-powered forecasting and generative AI for CFOs. CEO Shea stated 'AI is now at the core of nearly everything we do' [3]. Market Share (2/2): OneStream is recognized as the category leader in unified financial planning and close with 1,700+ customers including 18% of Fortune 500 companies, winning against Oracle, SAP, and Workday in competitive evaluations [1]. Pipeline (1/2): ESG Reporting & Planning solution launched to capture regulatory-driven demand (EU CSRD), SensibleAI product expansion continues, with CEO Shea noting the AI landscape in finance 'will be defined in 24-36 months' [2][3]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Heavy R&D investment driving the SensibleAI portfolio and platform extensibility; the company restructured its entire leadership around an AI-first go-to-market approach in December 2025 [3].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): OneStream's purpose — 'to be the operating system for modern finance' — is clear and identifies what the company does for whom, but remains a product-focused mission rather than a deeper societal purpose beyond business efficiency [3]. Founder-CEO Shea's vision 'to change the entire CPM ecosystem with a solution that combines power and flexibility with ease of use' is specific but product-centric. Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of notable charitable programs, corporate foundation, or community investment initiatives found. Employee Retention (1/2): CEO Tom Shea's 14-year tenure as founder provides leadership continuity; the December 2025 leadership restructuring promoting Scott Leshinski to President was orderly [3]. Employees cite 'great culture' and 'cool people' as positives, though salary competitiveness and micromanagement are flagged as concerns. Insider Buying (1/2): Founder-CEO Tom Shea directly owned 12.7% of the company's shares worth $729M (pre-acquisition), demonstrating significant personal conviction, though this reflects founder equity rather than recent open-market purchases [2].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored as Growth sleeve using pre-acquisition data. OneStream's balance sheet was clean with D/E of just 2.5%, providing financial flexibility [4]. Revenue growth was exceptional at 23.6% YoY to $602M in FY2025, with subscription revenue of $550M up 28% [1]. However, the company remained unprofitable with -8.4% profit margin and -12.4% ROE, reflecting heavy investment in growth and the AI platform transition. Forward P/E of 51.1x was elevated, scoring poorly against the 20x target. GAAP operating margin improved dramatically from -36% to -3% in Q4 2025, suggesting a path to profitability [1]. ARR of $699M (up 23%) and RPO of $1.4B (up 25%) demonstrate strong recurring revenue quality and forward visibility. Note: Stock was delisted April 1, 2026 following Hg acquisition completion; pre-acquisition avg daily volume of 4.5M shares at $24.00 = $109M daily, clearing the liquidity floor [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (4/9): OneStream operates in enterprise finance software — a category facing significant AI disruption pressure from much larger incumbents. Microsoft (Copilot for Finance), SAP, Oracle, and Workday are all integrating AI deeply into their finance platforms, and Big Four firms are building AI-powered finance automation [2][3]. CEO Shea explicitly acknowledged the existential stakes, stating the AI landscape in finance will produce 'emerging winners and losers' in the next 24-36 months [3]. OneStream's proprietary data models, 1,700+ customer relationships, and enterprise switching costs provide some protection, but the company's $602M revenue base is dwarfed by competitors with orders of magnitude more resources. The decision to go private reflects urgency to accelerate AI investment without public market constraints. AI Opportunity (1/1): OneStream generates quantifiable AI revenue — SensibleAI bookings grew 60% YoY in 2025's first nine months, and the SensibleAI portfolio (Agents, Studio, Forecast) is shipping to enterprise customers with measurable adoption [2][3].

infinite_game.summary: OneStream under founder-CEO Tom Shea demonstrates genuine long-term orientation — the decision to go private via the $6.4B Hg deal explicitly prioritizes AI investment over quarterly earnings pressure, and Shea stated the deal will allow OneStream to 'focus more aggressively on AI-driven innovation with a long-term focus rather than a quarterly one' [3]. However, the company's 14-year history is relatively short for evaluating infinite game durability, the purpose remains product-centric rather than cause-driven, and the competitive landscape demands sustained innovation against much larger rivals.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): OneStream's purpose — becoming 'the operating system for modern finance' — is a competitive ambition rather than a Just Cause in Sinek's terms [3]. It describes market dominance, not a vision of a better future that invites sacrifice and contribution beyond the company. The company exists to replace legacy finance tools with better ones, which is valuable but not inspiring beyond the customer base. Shea's vision 'to change the entire CPM ecosystem' is ambitious but remains product-focused.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): The most significant courageous decision was choosing to go private at $6.4B to accelerate AI investment rather than managing to quarterly earnings expectations [2][3]. CEO Shea stated the deal will allow 'focus more aggressively on AI-driven innovation with a long-term focus rather than a quarterly one.' Accepting PE ownership constraints to gain strategic freedom is a meaningful trade-off. The leadership restructuring promoting Scott Leshinski to President with an AI-first mandate also shows willingness to reorganize around long-term capability.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CEO Shea's 14-year founder tenure provides organizational stability, and the December 2025 leadership restructuring was orderly with internal promotions [3]. The company has grown rapidly from startup to 1,600+ employees, maintaining generally positive employee sentiment. However, some employee reviews flag micromanagement and below-market compensation concerns, suggesting cultural unevenness as the organization scaled.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): OneStream has evolved from an on-premise CPM tool to a cloud-native AI-enabled finance platform, and the decision to go private via the Hg acquisition represents a strategic bet on accelerating AI transformation without public market constraints [2][3]. The pivot to SensibleAI as the core value proposition and AI-first go-to-market represent meaningful evolution. However, the company has remained enterprise finance software since founding — adaptation within its category rather than transformative reinvention.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): OneStream explicitly acknowledges the strength of competitors like Microsoft, SAP, Oracle, and Workday in its competitive positioning [3]. CEO Shea's comments about 'emerging winners and losers' in AI-driven finance reflect awareness that competitors' AI capabilities push OneStream to innovate faster. The Gartner positioning language focuses on delivering customer value rather than destroying rivals. The decision to go private was partly driven by the need to compete more aggressively against better-resourced rivals.

investment_thesis: NOTE: OneStream was acquired by Hg Capital on April 1, 2026 at $24/share ($6.4B equity value) and is no longer publicly traded. This analysis reflects the company's last available public data and is provided for reference only. OneStream demonstrated strong category leadership — 5x Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader, 24% YoY revenue growth to $602M in FY2025, subscription revenue of $550M (up 28%), and a rapidly scaling SensibleAI portfolio with 60% YoY AI bookings growth [1][2]. GAAP operating margin improved from -36% to -3% in Q4 2025, suggesting an inflection toward profitability. ARR of $699M and RPO of $1.4B provided strong forward revenue visibility [1]. Founder-CEO Tom Shea's 14-year tenure and 12.7% ownership aligned leadership with long-term value creation.
thesis_continued: The acquisition by Hg removes OneStream from public market evaluation and limits ongoing transparency. At the time of the deal, the key risks included sustained unprofitability (-8.4% profit margin, -12.4% ROE), intense AI competitive pressure from Microsoft Copilot for Finance, SAP, Oracle, and Workday — all with vastly greater resources — and the existential uncertainty CEO Shea acknowledged about 'winners and losers' emerging in the AI finance landscape within 24-36 months [2][3]. The Hg deal validated the platform's strategic value at 10.6x EV/revenue but also reflected urgency to invest in AI capabilities without quarterly earnings scrutiny, suggesting the path to profitability requires sustained investment that may not have been well-received in public markets.
key_catalysts: COMPLETED: Hg Capital acquisition at $24/share closed April 1, 2026, providing $6.4B exit valuation and enabling accelerated AI investment as a private company [2]
SensibleAI portfolio expansion with 60% YoY AI bookings growth, 30+ plug-and-play AI routines, and SensibleAI Agents driving customer expansion and net retention [3]
Path to profitability — GAAP operating margin improved from -36% to -3% in Q4 2025, with subscription gross margin of 76% providing leverage as scale increases [1]
key_risks: No longer publicly traded — the Hg acquisition completed April 1, 2026, removing public market liquidity and ongoing transparency for outside investors
AI competitive pressure from Microsoft, SAP, Oracle, and Workday — all integrating AI into finance platforms with resources orders of magnitude greater than OneStream's $602M revenue base [3]

Sustained unprofitability — negative profit margin and ROE after 14 years suggest the business model requires continued heavy investment, with profitability timeline uncertain under private ownership [1]
sources: OneStream Q4 and Full-Year 2025 Financial Results — Revenue $602M, Subscription Revenue $550M, ARR $699M, February 2026
OneStream-Hg Capital Acquisition — $6.4B All-Cash Deal ($24/share), Announced January 2026, Completed April 1, 2026
OneStream AI Strategy, Leadership Updates, CEO Tom Shea Commentary — Fortune, PR Newswire, December 2025-January 2026
Yahoo Finance / Stock Analysis — OS Key Statistics (pre-delisting data), accessed April 2026
